# Drill-down brief - within-country equity: NGA

*Stunting (% of children under 5) by household wealth quintile, 2018. Generated by CEDAR - every figure cited below.*

## Key findings

- [!] Children in the **poorest fifth are stunted at 55.4%** versus **16.8% in the richest** - a **3.3x gap** (38.6 percentage points). [1]
- [i] The national average of **36.8%** masks this gradient: the poorest children fare far worse than the headline suggests. [2]
- [i] Stunting falls step-by-step across wealth groups (55.4% -> 49.4% -> 37.8% -> 26.9% -> 16.8%) - a socioeconomic gradient to target. [1]
- [caveat] Survey snapshot (2018 DHS/MICS), not an annual series; disaggregated estimates carry wider uncertainty than national totals.

## Methods & reproducibility

Drill-down = the same evidence chain at finer grain: discover wealth-disaggregated indicators -> retrieve each quintile with its own provenance -> verify coverage -> analyse the gap (ratio = poorest/richest; gap = difference in points). No figure is model-generated.
